named_buyer_examples: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (sells magnets to Realta, WHAM)
painful_job: HTS coils have very high minimum quench energy and slow normal-zone propagation, so voltage-tap detection fails; builders must choose between no-insulation windings (poor ramp/control) or unproven fast detection, over-engineering every multi-megajoule magnet (L03-004, L03-018, L03-020, L03-021).
product: Retrofittable quench detection + protection package: Rayleigh-backscatter fiber co-wound in the coil plus RF/acoustic hotspot channels, fused in a deterministic FPGA trigger driving a qualified multi-kV energy-extraction/CLIQ-class dump circuit; delivered with test data at 10-20 T relevant conditions. Merged extensions: delivered with a published acceptance-test protocol and warranty-support framing for merchant magnet sales (from A-04); the accumulated quench-precursor signature dataset across driven-quench campaigns is the compounding moat (from E-07).
frontier_vision: Become the certification-grade 'flight recorder and ejection seat' for every high-field HTS magnet -- fusion, NMR, accelerator, industrial -- the way rad-hard PLCs became mandatory in nuclear I&C.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: university magnet-lab access (e.g., high-field magnet facility) and instrumentation of small REBCO pancakes; publish detection-latency benchmarks. 2027-28: DOE SBIR/INFUSE-class grants with a fusion partner; controlled-quench campaigns on 1-2 T coils. 2029: demonstrate 100 ms-class pre-quench warning plus automated dump on a multi-kA coil at a partner lab; freeze v1 architecture and qualification plan.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: SPARC-class results in 2026-27 convert into pilot-plant magnet builds around 2030; every new builder needs protection it cannot buy today, and qualification data accumulated 2027-2029 is the moat at exactly that moment.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Post-SPARC fusion pilot-plant magnet procurement (CFS ARC-class, Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS follow-on) and the DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program renewal decision after its FY2027 authorization window (L03-032, L03-033, L03-035); CFETR magnet procurement if it opens (L03-024) is upside, not baseline. Side market (from E-07): KEPCO + LS Cable/LS Electric superconducting datacenter-grid MOU as a cable-monitoring channel (L03-042/043); US-ITER/ORNL magnet operations as lab-side buyers (L03-030). CN chapter (from C-17): ASIPP/CFETR demand logic exists (L03-024/029/037) but is served only via license-out and ONLY if export counsel clears - quench protection is dual-use-adjacent; no CN beachhead, flags unchanged.
competition_outlook_2030: In-house teams at CFS/TE Magnetics (L03-035, L03-044), national-lab solutions (CERN CLIQ lineage, tuned for LTS), instrumentation vendors without protection power electronics. No merchant vendor of an integrated HTS detection+dump product identified in the atlas.
timing_window_risk: If no-insulation windings prove fully sufficient at pilot-plant scale, the detection problem shrinks; if a magnet builder open-sources a good solution, differentiation collapses to service/qualification.
extreme_edge: Watching a superconductor start to die in real time -- centimeter-resolved strain/temperature along kilometers of tape at kilohertz rates -- and killing a 100 MJ discharge before it burns the coil.
first_experiment: Co-wind fiber + RF pickup in a 1-2 T REBCO double-pancake, drive controlled quenches, and demonstrate >=100 ms warning before thermal runaway with <1% false-trigger rate (2027, university magnet lab).
secondary_markets: JP: Furukawa/Fujikura REBCO ecosystem as conductor-QA partners (L03-044) [from E-07]
KR: KEPCO + LS Cable datacenter superconducting-grid MOU as cable-monitoring side market (L03-042, L03-043) [from E-07]
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: distributed fiber-optic sensing readout, multi-channel fusion in a deterministic FPGA trigger, and qualified energy-extraction electronics map directly onto founder instrumentation, embedded-systems, circuits, and data-acquisition skills.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-04 (acceptance-test protocol / warranty framing for merchant magnet sales; multi-modal sensing sources L03-005/006/007/008), C-17 (CN ASIPP/CFETR chapter - documented as license-out-only, counsel-gated; china_beachhead stays false), and E-07's protection half (precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS cable-monitoring side market; its QA-bench half went to D-02).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical quench detection/protection. Falsifiable 2027 experiment; honest NI-winding substitution risk. Absorb A-04 protocol framing, E-07 precursor-dataset moat + KEPCO/LS side market; CN only if counsel clears.

concept: Reel-to-reel contactless REBCO tape quality metrology (Hall-array critical-current mapping + delamination screening) sold to tape vendors and magnet builders as certified acceptance instrumentation.
named_buyer_examples: Furukawa/SuperPower (several-hundred-km supply deal to Tokamak Energy)
Shanghai Superconductor / Shanghai Shangchuang (CN domestic capacity buildout)
Western Superconducting Technologies (CN, ITER strand lineage, L03-031, from B-10)
ASIPP Hefei high-field magnet program (CN, L03-029/037, from B-10)
painful_job: Fewer than 10 tape vendors supply the world; a single fusion order absorbs a meaningful share of annual output, yet Ic uniformity, strain/delamination and screening-current degradation force magnet builders to over-specify and re-test tape with bespoke, non-comparable acceptance methods (L03-003, L03-019, L03-018, L03-020, L03-021).
product: 77 K reel-to-reel scanner: dense Hall-sensor array plus lock-in thermal imaging maps position-resolved Ic and detects buried delamination/dropouts at m/s line speed, with transport-Ic-calibrated reporting and a shared acceptance data format for vendor and buyer. Merged extensions: 10kA-class cable/CICC acceptance stations and financier/insurer-grade data packages (from C-11); per-meter 'conductor passport' reporting framing (from B-10); conductor/joint QA benches with a Furukawa/Fujikura partnership channel (from E-07).
frontier_vision: The KLA of superconductors: every kilometer of HTS tape and every finished cable/coil ships with a standardized, instrument-traceable quality map, closing the loop back into deposition process control.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: benchtop magnetscan rig on purchased tape lots; publish correlation of contactless Ic estimate to transport Ic within a few percent. 2027-28: pilot placements with one US magnet builder and one tape vendor under NRE contracts; extend to coil/cable acceptance heads. 2029: inline-speed prototype at a vendor line; draft an open acceptance-report schema with two counterparties.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Tape volume triples-to-10x between 2026 and the early-2030s pilot-plant builds; QC pain scales with volume and with new entrants' unproven lines, peaking exactly at launch.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Fusion pilot-plant tape procurement waves (CFS ARC-class; Tokamak Energy ST80-HTS wire deal as precedent, L03-044), Chinese domestic REBCO capacity expansion for import independence (L03-052), and CFETR magnet procurement if construction starts (L03-024). Additional evidenced demand (merged): DOE milestone-program QA needs and CFS magnet-as-product certification (L03-032/035, from C-11); the State Grid Shanghai 35 kV commercial HTS cable and follow-ons (L03-041, from B-10); ASIPP's high-field program (L03-029/037) and WST's ITER-strand QA lineage (L03-031).
competition_outlook_2030: Vendor in-house scanners (THEVA Tapestar-class -- judgment), Lake Shore-style lab instruments, national-lab one-offs. No merchant, buyer-trusted acceptance-metrology vendor in the atlas record set.
timing_window_risk: Tape vendors may bundle 'good-enough' in-house QC data; if one instrument maker partners exclusively with the largest vendor, the neutral-referee position is lost.
extreme_edge: Imaging supercurrents through millimeters of steel and silver at highway speed -- a contactless ammeter for a quantum fluid.
first_experiment: Benchtop 77 K reel-to-reel Hall-array scan of 100 m commercial tape; blind-correlate predicted vs transport Ic on 50 cut samples to <5% error (2027).
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: Hall-array magnetic sensing, lock-in thermal imaging, and line-speed data acquisition with calibrated reporting are core founder sensors/instrumentation, electromagnetics, and numerical-analysis work.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed C-11 (cable/CICC acceptance stations, financier/insurer data packages), B-10 (conductor-passport framing + CN buyer set: WST, ASIPP), and E-07's QA-bench half (conductor/joint benches, Furukawa/Fujikura JP channel). China leg already primary (US+CN, china_beachhead true) and strengthened with eligible CN evidence (L03-041/029/037/031) - flags unchanged.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Best instrument seed in pool; demand scales with conductor volume not fusion success (low-beta). Absorb C-11 cable acceptance stations + insurer packages, B-10 passport framing + CN buyers, E-07 QA-bench half.

concept: RF energy recovery for the world's klystron and gyrotron fleets: multi-stage depressed collector (MSDC) modules plus adaptive drive electronics that close the 12-point gap between simulated and hardware RF efficiency.
named_buyer_examples: DOE accelerator labs (SLAC GREEN-RF commercialization precedent)
fusion ECH system integrators buying ITER-class gyrotrons
painful_job: Most accelerator RF sources run below 50% wall-plug efficiency (L05-039); the 2015 bunching technique simulated >90% klystron efficiency but funded hardware reached only 78.5% (L05-011, L05-012, L05-013); spent-beam energy is dumped as heat, costing large facilities millions annually in electricity and cooling.
product: Retrofit-at-rebuild MSDC collector assemblies plus gun/drive waveform optimization electronics, supplied to tube OEMs and lab rebuild shops, with guaranteed wall-plug efficiency uplift measured on a standard test protocol.
frontier_vision: Every megawatt tube ships energy-recovering by default; the same collector/drive stack later powers fusion-plant heating systems where recirculating power directly sets plant economics.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: beam-optics and collector design study anchored on a decommissioned S-band klystron; engage SLAC-lineage GREEN-RF collaborators (L05-032). 2027-28: collector prototype on a lab test stand; measure recovered power vs simulation. 2029: retrofit demonstration on an operating test-stand tube with published before/after wall-plug data. FIX additions: 2026-27 includes fleet-size and $-per-efficiency-point arithmetic from named facilities (SLAC-class linacs, ITER-class gyrotron stations) to bound the retrofit TAM; 2028: OEM/rebuilder channel agreement in principle (CPI-class); 2029 DECISION POINT: license the MSDC IP to tube OEMs or fold the drive-electronics line into P3R2-C-09 - a standalone company only if both the channel agreement and the arithmetic clear.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Facility electricity costs and decarbonization targets make efficiency retrofits fundable; rebuild cycles of aging tube fleets 2030-2034 are the natural insertion point without new-tube qualification risk.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: DOE GREEN-RF-style commercialization push (L05-032), fusion ECH procurement following the ITER/Thales gyrotron template as pilot plants order heating systems (L05-029), and collider-class efficiency programs (CEPC's documented ~RMB130M/yr savings case, L05-013 -- China demand logic, access-limited).
competition_outlook_2030: CPI (L05-050), Thales (L05-029), Canon (judgment), Vitzro Nextec (KR, L05-037) building their own high-efficiency tubes; labs doing one-off in-house collectors. Merchant retrofit modules with guaranteed uplift are unoccupied.
timing_window_risk: Invasive retrofits only work at rebuild; if OEMs internalize MSDC quickly the wedge narrows to drive electronics and test protocols.
extreme_edge: Catching a spent electron beam on a staircase of precisely biased electrodes and pushing its energy back into the wall plug.
first_experiment: MSDC prototype on a decommissioned S-band klystron at a lab test stand: demonstrate >=15-point wall-plug efficiency uplift vs baseline at 50%+ of rated output (2028).
founder_fit_note: Electron-optics simulation and drive-waveform electronics draw on founder electromagnetics, numerical-analysis, and circuits skills, but vacuum-tube fabrication, collector thermal design, and rebuild-shop integration are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: named-facility fleet/$-per-efficiency-point arithmetic task added; 2028 OEM/rebuilder channel-agreement-in-principle milestone; explicit 2029 license-or-fold-into-C-09 decision. Licensing-shaped structure acknowledged (retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization risk).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Non-obvious and documented gap, but retrofit-at-rebuild + OEM internalization makes it licensing-shaped.

concept: Compact solid-state-RF-driven e-beam/X-ray sterilization machine that breaks the IBA/L3 duopoly with a self-shielded, factory-installable unit for the ethylene-oxide phase-out.
painful_job: EtO replacement demand has the two pure-play e-beam accelerator makers (IBA Industrial, L3 Applied Technologies) backlogged for years, leaving medical-device and food producers unable to add sterilization capacity on schedule (L05-028); revised ISO 11137-1:2025 tightens radiation-sterilization validation (L05-042).
product: Self-shielded 10 MeV/30 kW-class e-beam (with X-ray conversion option) built on mass-produced GaN/LDMOS solid-state RF pallets instead of klystrons, shippable in standard modules, with dose-mapping automation aligned to ISO 11137-1:2025 out of the box. Deployment options (FIX, absorbing A-09): the same accelerator/RF core ships either as a central contract-sterilization machine or as a compact self-shielded factory-floor cell for device-OEM in-house sterilization (conveyor + integrated shielding + ISO 11137 dosimetry records) - acknowledging that per-site radiation licensing and contract-sterilization industry structure favor the central variant first.
frontier_vision: Sterilization as an appliance: distributed, per-factory beam processing replacing centralized gamma/EtO plants, then food-safety and materials-modification markets on the same platform.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: SSPA-driven accelerating-structure design with a university accelerator lab; regulatory mapping (ISO 11137/FDA pathways). 2027-28: 1 MeV/5 kW prototype beamline validating SSPA drive, gun, and dose uniformity. 2029: 10 MeV injector section + shielding/throughput engineering package; LOIs from two contract sterilizers. FIX gates: 2027 backlog-persistence check (kill if IBA/L3 clear backlogs and cut prices); 2028 SSPA-vs-klystron cost-crossover validation with vendor quotes; P4 re-source of the EtO regulatory driver from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries (the current ledger lacks it).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Working hypothesis with three load-bearing assumptions, each gated (FIX): (1) the EtO forcing function is durable regulatory pressure, not a one-cycle backlog - must be re-sourced from eligible primary documents (EPA NESHAP amendments / FDA sterilization guidance) in P4; (2) solid-state RF cost curves cross klystron economics before 2030 - validate with vendor quotes by 2028; (3) incumbent backlog persists - 2027 check: if IBA/L3 backlogs clear and prices fall, kill.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Continued EtO-replacement capacity build with duopoly backlog precedent (L05-028), ISO 11137-1:2025 revalidation cycles (L05-042), and cargo/industrial linac demand growth signaling accelerator supply tightness (L05-033); China's CGN Dasheng RMB340M order flow proves category economics (L05-035, CN market evidence -- CN treated as competitor territory, not beachhead).
competition_outlook_2030: IBA, L3 Applied Technologies (backlogged incumbents), Mevex (judgment), CGN Dasheng expanding from China (L05-035); Co-60 gamma remains substitute with its own supply constraints.
timing_window_risk: If incumbent backlogs clear by 2029 and they cut prices, a new entrant faces entrenched service networks; regulatory qualification timelines could push first revenue past 2031.
extreme_edge: A shipping-container linac: thousands of transistor watts phase-summed into a 10 MeV electron broom that sterilizes a pallet in seconds.
first_experiment: SSPA-driven 1 MeV/5 kW CW prototype beamline: demonstrate stable dose rate and RF-chain wall-plug efficiency >55% (2028, university accelerator facility).
founder_fit_note: Solid-state RF drive, dose-mapping automation, and machine controls fit founder circuits, controls, and instrumentation skills, while accelerator physics, radiation shielding, and sterilization regulatory validation are outside the profile.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: EtO forcing function flagged for P4 re-sourcing from EPA NESHAP/FDA primaries; SSPA-vs-klystron cost crossover to be validated with quotes by 2028; 2027 backlog-persistence kill check added; A-09's self-shielded in-house cell absorbed as a deployment option (with its licensing-friction caveat).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Canonical sterilization machine; duopoly opening real but three load-bearing assumptions unproven.

concept: Charge-buildup-immune beam current and dose metrology for ultra-high-dose-rate (FLASH) and industrial electron beams -- the traceable ammeter the field admits it lacks.
named_buyer_examples: industrial e-beam sterilization operators needing ISO 11137 dose traceability
painful_job: Two independent 2024-2025 groups showed beam current transformers are corrupted by electric-field-driven charge buildup at FLASH dose rates -- an unresolved calibration/traceability problem for both clinical and industrial e-beam (L05-023, L05-024); sterilization validation standards meanwhile tighten (L05-042).
product: Hybrid non-intercepting monitor: cavity-resonator charge measurement cross-calibrated against an integrated Faraday/calorimetric reference, with metrology-grade uncertainty budget and drop-in form factors replacing legacy BCTs (Bergoz-class footprint). Merged extension (from A-08): a sterilization-QA product tier - per-unit calibration certificates for e-beam/X-ray plants validating under ISO 11137-1:2025 - alongside the clinical FLASH tier.
frontier_vision: Own the traceability layer for every high-dose-rate beam on earth -- clinical FLASH, sterilization, cargo scanning, semiconductor e-beam -- then expand into full beam-diagnostics suites.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: uncertainty-budget design and cavity prototype; beam time via university medical-physics collaborations. 2027-28: side-by-side campaign (cavity vs BCT vs Faraday cup) on a FLASH-capable linac, published. 2029: pre-production units at two beta sites (one clinical program, one industrial e-beam plant); NIST-traceable calibration chain drafted.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: FLASH clinical translation and e-beam sterilization expansion both hit regulatory dose-traceability walls around 2030; a validated instrument with published uncertainty is ready exactly when device makers must specify one.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: FLASH radiotherapy device qualification campaigns (pain documented in L05-023/024), EtO-replacement e-beam capacity requiring ISO 11137-1:2025-compliant dosimetry (L05-028, L05-042).
competition_outlook_2030: Bergoz Instrumentation (entrenched, published price list, L05-049), in-house lab diagnostics; incumbents optimized for storage rings, not UHDR transients.
timing_window_risk: Niche instrument market; if FLASH stalls clinically, industrial e-beam alone caps the market at tens of millions.
extreme_edge: Weighing 10^11 electrons in a microsecond without touching them, to a certified percent.
first_experiment: Side-by-side cavity monitor vs BCT vs Faraday cup at a FLASH-capable electron linac; quantify BCT error vs dose rate and demonstrate <1% cavity-monitor agreement with calorimetry (2027).
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: cavity-resonator charge measurement, Faraday/calorimetric cross-calibration, and metrology-grade uncertainty budgets are precision instrumentation and electromagnetics work at the center of the founder profile.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-08 (ISO 11137-1:2025 sterilization-QA channel and calibration-certificate product tier). C-10's CN leg NOT imported (mirrored assertion without CN procurement/regulatory evidence - 'fake dual' per adjudication); D-09 remains US-primary.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical UHDR metrology. Documented unresolved failure, cheap decisive experiment, honest small-TAM admission; pairs with C-09/D-08.

concept: Coherent-beam-combining phase-control engine: a channel-scalable electro-optic 'conductor' (controller ASIC/FPGA + modulator front-ends) sold to directed-energy primes and ultrafast-laser OEMs.
named_buyer_examples: nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Martin Aculight (JLWS awardees)
painful_job: Transverse mode instability caps single-fiber power, making coherent combining of many channels the only path to 300-500 kW weapons and multi-kW ultrafast tools -- but multi-channel phase-locking, path-length and dispersion management remain bespoke, unsolved engineering rebuilt per program (L12-016, L12-017, L12-018, L12-001, L12-002).
product: Scalable phase-control stack: per-channel EO modulator front-ends, deterministic sub-microsecond controller (SPGD + interferometric hybrid) proven from 16 to 1,000+ channels, vibration-hardened, with open telemetry for system integration -- the subsystem every combiner needs and no one sells. Merged extension (from A-17): an environmental-grade deformable-mirror control and turbulence/jitter-compensation tier for 100kW-class systems, extending the phase-control engine from fiber arrays to full beam-control stacks.
frontier_vision: Be the Synopsys of coherent light: the standard control layer for laser weapons, ultrafast machining arrays, beamed power, and eventually phased optical apertures for space links.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-27: 16-channel testbed at a university photonics lab; lock to lambda/30 rms under acoustic disturbance. 2027-28: SBIR/STTR under DEW-adjacent topics; 128-channel demonstration with combining efficiency >90%. 2029: ruggedized brassboard evaluated by one prime under CRADA/sub-contract; ASIC path defined.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: JLWS transitions from initial award (2026) to scaled 300-500 kW fielding decisions in the early 2030s; primes under schedule pressure buy proven subsystems rather than rebuild controls, exactly when a 2029-validated brassboard exists.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: JLWS production scaling toward its $847M ceiling (L12-031, L12-032), DARPA AMPED/MELT tile architectures maturing into acquisition (L12-040, L12-041), and industrial CBC ultrafast systems entering machining markets (L12-002). Additional demand leg (from A-17): NNSA Expanded Yield Capability (EYC) execution beyond CD-1 (L12-042) and DARPA MELT/POWER follow-ons (L12-047).
competition_outlook_2030: Prime in-house control teams (nLIGHT, LM -- L12-033), Civan Lasers' integrated OPA approach (judgment), academic spinouts; no merchant phase-control vendor identified in the atlas.
timing_window_risk: Primes may classify and internalize controls; mitigation is dual-use industrial sales and ITAR-segregated product lines.
extreme_edge: A thousand lasers made to march in phase to a thirtieth of a wavelength -- an orchestra conducted at petahertz pitch with microsecond reflexes.
first_experiment: 16-channel fiber array phase-locked to lambda/30 rms with >90% combining efficiency under 1 g random vibration (2027).
founder_fit_note: Strong transfer: per-channel electro-optic modulation, deterministic sub-microsecond FPGA control loops, and interferometric feedback are founder controls, embedded-systems, circuits, and signal-processing territory.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: absorbed A-17 (ruggedized deformable-mirror/adaptive-optics control tier; NNSA EYC demand leg L12-042 and MELT/POWER follow-ons L12-047; AO sources L12-009/010/011).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Canonical CBC phase control; right abstraction level; dual-use hedge vs prime NIH; realistic channel-count ladder. Absorb A-17 DM-control + NNSA leg.
